architecture.summary: ActPRM is an active learning framework for training Process Reward Models (PRMs) that trains an ensemble PRM with multiple classification heads on a shared LLM backbone. During training, the ensemble PRM estimates uncertainty (both aleatoric and epistemic) over candidate data after the forward pass. Samples with high uncertainty are selected and annotated by QwQ-32B, a capable reasoning model. Loss is computed only on labeled uncertain samples, and the PRM weights are updated. This selective annotation strategy reduces labeling costs while maintaining or improving PRM performance on step-level correctness classification in mathematical reasoning trajectories.
trained_components: ensemble PRM heads (multiple classification heads on shared backbone)
uncertainty thresholds (τ_aleatoric, τ_epistemic) via hyperparameter search

biblio.title: Efficient Process Reward Model Training via Active Learning

baseline.name: full data tuning on 100K labeled samples

claims.statement: ActPRM achieves an average F1 score on ProcessBench matching baseline performance while using only half the annotation budget in pool-based active learning.
ActPRM outperforms random selection by 3.3% at 50% annotation budget.
ActPRM achieves state-of-the-art performance of 75.0% on ProcessBench, outperforming Qwen2.5-Math-PRM-7B by 1.5% while requiring only 6% of its annotation budget.
ActPRM-X achieves new state-of-the-art performance of 76.0% on ProcessBench, outperforming Universal-PRM by 1.7%.
ActPRM reduces annotation costs by 47.0% by filtering over 1M+ math reasoning trajectories from NuminaMath, retaining 60% of the data (563,030 labeled points).
Epistemic and aleatoric uncertainty are complementary to each other in ActPRM's uncertainty estimation.

ActPRM outperforms random selection of the same amount of data, with model trained on ActPRM-selected data exhibiting consistently higher training loss (margin of 0.05), indicating the selected data is more challenging and informative.
